name: Embalse del Ebro – circular from Reinosa
name_original: Embalse del Ebro – circular desde Reinosa
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Campoo De Enmedio traverse a landscape defined by its mountainous character and significant elevation changes. The region features the Embalse del Ebro, providing routes alongside a large reservoir, and extends into higher elevations with notable climbs. Road cyclists can expect varied terrain, from valley roads to challenging mountain passes, offering diverse experiences. Best road cycling routes around Campoo De Enmedio The most popular road cycling route is Embalse del Ebro – circular from Reinosa, a 36.3 miles (58.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route circles the Embalse del Ebro, offering scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto Campoo - Fuente del Chivo Climb, a difficult 37.5 miles (60.3 km) path. This route features a significant ascent to Fuente del Chivo, providing challenging climbs and expansive mountain vistas. Local road cyclists also love the Nestares, Alto del Bardal, Mataporquera, Matamorosa – circular route, a 39.3 miles (63.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with moderate climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Campoo De Enmedio is defined by reservoir views, significant mountain climbs, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to advanced road cycling routes with climbs. The routes in Campoo De Enmedio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 40 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Campoo De Enmedio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: Campoo De Enmedio offers over 50 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a diverse network of trails, from moderate loops to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: Yes, Campoo De Enmedio has several routes suitable for beginners and families. There are 6 easy routes, and many of the 29 moderate routes can also be enjoyed by those looking for a less strenuous ride. The region's varied terrain ensures options for different ability levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: Road cycling routes in Campoo De Enmedio feature a diverse landscape. You can expect routes alongside the large Embalse del Ebro reservoir, offering scenic views, as well as higher elevations with significant climbs and rolling hills. The region is characterized by its mountainous nature.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: Absolutely. Campoo De Enmedio is known for its challenging mountain passes. There are 21 difficult routes, including paths with significant ascents like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005123044/alto-campoo-fuente-del-chivo-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Campoo - Fuente del Chivo Climb&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive mountain vistas after a demanding ascent.

Q: What are some popular circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Many road cyclists enjoy circular routes that showcase the region's beauty. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004565148/embalse-del-ebro-circular-from-reinosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro – circular from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the reservoir. Another well-liked loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005124609/nestares-alto-del-bardal-mataporquera-matamorosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nestares, Alto del Bardal, Mataporquera, Matamorosa – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain and moderate climbs.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the mountainous character of Campoo De Enmedio suggests that late spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the most favorable weather for road cycling, avoiding the harsher conditions of winter at higher elevations. Always check local forecasts before heading out.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: The road cycling routes in Campoo De Enmedio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from scenic reservoir views to challenging mountain climbs, and the variety of options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in points of interest. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, or traverse the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3277001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass&lt;/a&gt;. There are also beautiful sections through beech forests, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4628486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful section through the beech forest&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take to complete in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004565148/embalse-del-ebro-circular-from-reinosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro – circular from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, at 36.3 miles (58.4 km), typically takes about 2 hours 35 minutes. More challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005123044/alto-campoo-fuente-del-chivo-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Campoo - Fuente del Chivo Climb&lt;/a&gt;, which is 37.5 miles (60.3 km) with significant elevation, can take over 3 hours.

Q: Are there options for longer, more extensive road cycling tours in Campoo De Enmedio?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Campoo De Enmedio offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River – Alto del Bardal loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 83.8 km route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31480623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aurora Fountain – La Llanuca Roman Ruins loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 80 km, providing ample distance for endurance cyclists.

Q: What are the road conditions like in Campoo De Enmedio, especially in autumn?
A: Road conditions in Campoo De Enmedio are generally well-maintained for cycling. In autumn, you can expect cooler temperatures and potentially wet leaves or damp patches, especially in forested areas or after rain. Higher elevations might experience colder weather earlier in the season. Always exercise caution and be prepared for changing conditions.
tips: In "La Llanuca" are the large houses of the ancient city of Juliobriga from Roman times, about 1,200 m² and comparable to current mansions. These belonged to the upper class of the city.

The Tower of the Church of San Roque, popularly known as the Cathedral of the Fish, rises above the waters of the Ebro reservoir. With the construction of the reservoir, between 1921 and 1945, towns in the region, such as Medianedo, La Magdalena and Quintanilla, disappeared under its waters. A must visit for everyone visiting the area - the place is super nice and quiet.
Nice road bike route bordering the Ebro reservoir, Concorte, Cantabria.
It is freely visited, next to the church.
